author: @NewWorldHoarder
text:
Like many here, I only found this out by watching the more recent video where you are out on your pushbike. 

I’m so sorry for your loss, I can’t imagine what it’s like. 

Thank you for inspiring me last winter to mess about with alternative heating sources when my boiler broke and I couldn’t afford a new one. 

I haven’t burnt my house down yet lol. 

P.S. Best result was an empty (new) wood burner with a stainless steel beaker (or two) filled with vermiculite and methanol. Got warm pretty fast and stayed hot for a while after the fuel ran out. Perfect for a freezing cold hallway! 🙂

R.I.P. Patti. You were obviously a delightful person and great partner for Rob. ❤

May God bless and keep you.

author: @garymatysik
text:
Dear Robert, I like everyone was sad to hear of your and your family's loss and I hope that you will soon find some sort of peace. I can only speak for myself, but your loss has been felt by myself in an unexpected way, every morning I shared my breakfast with you for so long that you became part of my life.  
To start each with genuine kindness and intelligence set me up for the rest of the day, and that is now missing, gone.
Perhaps there is a follow video to this one, where you let everyone know how you are doing, and I have just not found it? Please let US know how you are doing, as you are important to many in many ways.
Kindest Regards

author: @nobye9716
text: Rob, you have the same name as my dad, and for the last few years, I have looked up to you like a dad, even though you don't know me. I am a 25 year old woman from Alberta, Canada, and my mom went missing 6 years ago. I know it is not the same, but please know that you are so appreciated and Patti is so loved by you and anyone who cares about you. I am so sorry for this situation, and I know it can't be easy right now. But the thing I try to remember, is that we have to keep living and experiencing life FOR the people we lose. There are too many beautiful sunsets and rises that we need to witness for them. Remember your channels main goal of empowerment, and how you and Patti both have empowered SO many people to do things themselves and keep learning and experimenting and becoming self-sufficient. What you have built is beautiful! Nobody expects you to bounce right back, we all need time and patience. If there is anything that I can help with, please let me know. I am proficient in computers and would be happy to volunteer my time and skills to help with anything you might need, wishing the absolute best for you and your family today.

author: @anthonys8422
text:
In the gentle embrace of silence, I found myself wandering through the corridors of cyberspace, seeking the familiar glow of your creations. Amidst the void, a whisper of concern stirred within me, prompting a pilgrimage to your digital sanctuary. Discovering this lamentation alongside your faithful companion cast a somber shadow upon my soul.

Your laughter, a melodic symphony of self-contentment, echoes through the ether, a healing elixir for the weary souls who seek solace in pixels and screens. May your hiatus be but a fleeting interlude, a momentary pause in the grand symphony of your creations. 

Behold, the humble abode of the mad scientist in his garage, who stands as a beacon of ingenuity amidst the vast sea of content. Your channel, a crucible of inspiration and enlightenment, bestows upon its audience the gift of contemplation, a legacy that shall endure through the ebb and flow of time.

author: @faithgrace4690
text: Praying for you and your family during this incredibly hard time. Praying God's strength, comfort, peace, courage, and love to uphold you all and be known mightily and experienced by you all. During prayer, for my loss, I heard this and pray it comforts you all too: "When we know the LORD, we have this great hope of never dying but living in HIM for eternity . We need to allow God's love and grace to meet us, as it ushers this knowledge of HIM and HIS WORD and PROMISES- Allow the Holy Spirit to reveal HIS peace and be one with you - knowing where she is and that this time of loss is temporary. (Not belittling the pain or grief, but we redirect and celebrate our loved one's life, all we experienced with them, where they are and the future.) We also need to change our perspective to align with HIS, which will show us that each day is a day closer to being with them again- instead of without them." I pray this helps you all too. Don't allow this to distance you from Our LORD, but draw closer to HIM, trusting in HIS love to keep you strengthened all the more, resting in HIS love. Sending you love and continual prayers . 🙏🙏💜💜

author: @stephendixon8575
text:
Hey Rob, it just so happens that I found this video after discovering your channel (from watching your video about voltage regulators) and then first watching your “What’s this channel all about” video. Your boundless positivity and inspiring monologue explaining that the aim of the channel is really all about empowerment really put a huge smile on my face and I immediately hit ‘Subscribe’. So to then see the list of videos and find the first one explained there had been such a huge, sad loss in your life so recently was quite a bombshell. 

Even though we’ve never met, and I’ve literally only just found your channel today, I can see from the size subscriptions it’s obvious that your inspiring positivity has made you and Patti very much loved ❤by so many people, and that you’ve created quite a legacy for you both here! I can only hope that this thought can give you a tiny bit of comfort at a time if and when you may need it. Keep on smiling as much as you can and just keep on ‘buggering on’ as they say. I hope all the positivity you’ve been radiating into the World for so long can generate a little sunshine for you keep the clouds away🤞

Of course, my prayers join those of your family and friends at this time. May you find great solace in the memories and your faith, as you continue on; take the time to look after yourself A little extra well, and your research journey and teaching would be here for you when you’re ready for it again.❤

author: @flymypg
text:
Thank you for sharing this video together with the news of Patti's passing. The yin-yang of joy and sorrow does not blend to make gray, but invites us to simultaneously hold them both in our mind and heart to create comfort rather than contradiction or dissonance. While the future will create no new memories of Patti, all of the past memories still exist. This post emphasizes that dichotomy, the middle way being to bring all the past memories into the future with us, to perhaps help balance her loss.

Thanks for illustrating this so clearly, for letting us share a sliver of your joy and loss.

author: @gmosc
text:
I must comment to keep this alive. 
Your laugh is missed. 
Your laugh was a sign of wonder, a sign of everlasting youthful joy, ...... And I hope you find an extra meaning in your own laugh where maybe it becomes a sign of you continung to embrace her love and wanting to share it with others. 
There was nothing trivial about your laugh. It invited us into your understanding of the world. And now, your understanding of the world is different, and..... your laugh will be deeper.
Some people laugh so hard that they cry. There must be a connection between the two.
I've never understood that.
I've heard it said that even though it feels impossible now, but there comes a time when remembering a loved one evokes  smile and a laugh along with the sorrow. The sorrow will always be there, but she was a hugely supportive figure in your life that encouraged your youthful wonderment. 
She has always been a part of your laugh. She will also continue to be a part of it when your laugh returns.
Hugs from all of us.

author: @HansHartman
text:
Robert,
I am sorry, so very sorry for your loss. May God take her into His light and bless her for all eternity. May the peace of the Lord Jesus Christ comfort you and your Son.
Know that the death of a person’s body is not the end. When I was 10, I had brain surgery and had a reaction to the dye they used. For about 3 minutes, I had no heart beat. But I saw the operating room and heard things. I also felt an incredible feeling of love and light behind me. I knew that if I turned around, I would have not made my way back to earthly life.
There is something after this life and it is eternal. I pray for you and yours this day. I will continue to pray for you.
As always, I thank you for sharing your life with so many strangers. You have made a positive impact on the world. Please know that you have a place under my roof as you are welcome.

author: @Will-d2g
text: I am truly sorry for your loss!  But don't worry about her, she is doing quite well; trust me.  I was in a 4 hour coma years ago and I died 4 times in one day, 2 years ago.  I learned thing that I couldn't have known in both.  She is doing well, but right now, she is learning and will be kept   busy learning all new things.  Right before bed, call out her name and say "come visit me in my dreams" and she will come when she has a moment to come.  When you see her don't waste time!  She only has what seems to be about 15 minutes, but in actuality, it's only about 15 seconds, so don't waste time!!!  Tell her you love her, you miss her, where are the keys to the car, whatever you want to say, but don't waste time!!!  The next night, you can do it again and so on.  I hope this helps, because it does work.  Just her body died, but her soul, life force, spirit whatever you want to call it, never dies!  I am not sorry for her, I envy her, but I am sorry for you, your family and friends, because all of you have to remain behind to continue on.  But I am sorry for your loss, I truly am!!!  God Bless All Of You!!!

author: @universebecomingltd
text: She had me giggling as she wrapped that tape!  hehehe! 😁😁 I never saw her until now, but just this short clip was all I needed to see what a charmer she was.  🙂  Being scientists and teachers I'm sure you two talked about life and death numerous times.  A scientist myself I've spent most of my life thus far trying to figure it all out.  My best explanation is that universe cannot destroy universe.  Thus, all that is will always be.  And so, I tend to view death as a temporary departure from this atomic realm.  And being that I can't find a way to explain how nothingness can spawn into somethingness I'll bet we've all been doing this forever and ever.  Not a quick fix for the sorrows of a loved one passing on because we are invested in life.  Invested, in this reality as we live life to the fullest.  Yet, over time, I think the scientist in you will see something similar, if not already.  I feel bad for you Rob because you're one of my favorite teachers and I have a fondness for you because of that.  The things I have learned from you over the years are a significant portion of my knowledge.  Thanks for sharing her with us.  Everything will be ok.  You'll see. 🙂

author: @MistahBufo
text:
Youtube algorithm brought me here. Commenting and liking. Sorry for your loss, sir. I hope you can take comfort in the many long years you got to spend with her, doing the thing you both clearly so dearly loved to do.

Losing your life partner is possibly one of the hardest things in life to go through. An unfathomable pain that rips and tears and shreds the heart. I don't believe in god, per se--I am not a spiritual man. But I believe in a circle of life and rebirth, a natural order of things. The natural order of things brought you together and it pulls you apart, as happens with all things. But the universe could never have accounted for all the memories you made together, and through them she lives on within you and all whom she knew. I hope you can find the time to get together and reminisce about her, often, in the years to follow. <3 Sorry for your loss.

author: @MarkBanister
text: As surely devastating as you and your family must feel at her loss, it is imperative that you reflect on all the joy and happiness that she elected to share with you, and you with her as that can never be lost. Be and remain strong for your own sake, but most especially for hers for to quote something you know.... "it is better to have loved and lost than never to have loved at all.".... And in your understandable grief always remember that she elected to spend her precious time with you..... what a generous gift freely given. Bless you both, your family and friends, and when your able return to your shared passion... educating, enlightening and enriching our lives with your knowledge and enthusiasm. Be well, be strong and thank you (both)!

author: @derphyn
text: How dark the days; how lonely the nights.  Life seeming to be a dream in which one will wake up and find everything as it was - and then rediscovering she's gone.  Times of deep feeling, then numbness; work giving a respite for a time, only to find at day's end there's a dark emptiness where she had been.  Though all of us who watch your channel are far away, and may never meet you face-to-face, we are with you in your aloneness.  May you be surrounded by people who care about you during this dark time.  Take as much time as you need to take care of yourself, mate.

author: @Papadum1950
text: Dear Robert and Family, I think I speak for many quiet, but inspired members when I say that we are devastated by your loss. All we would want, is for you to have all the time you need to process your grief, to live through your memories, to gather together the remnants of your own soul after a big hole has been torn out of it. We will quietly and reverently, carry on with our own little efforts to be enthusiastic about life and science and discovery until you can find your own feet again. We are all there for you. We expect very little now that your muse has gone, but we will all be there when you feel strong enough to get back to all of us.

text: You are lucky.  Not were.  Are.  She will be with you… always.  The video shows her to be a kindred spirit. Memories that currently cause heartache will, in time, trigger secret smiles and warm fuzzies.  And laughter.  Your genuine gleeful guffaws are something she must have been proud to have inspired and delighted to have shared.  My condolences.

author: @OneOfAMineRocks
text:
What are perception of death is, well that perception is WRONG!
How do "i" know, I don't, but my higher power does, they guided me here at this moment in time, to say that, I didn't plan it/this, it's just me following what I'm supposed to, just me listening to that voice!
This is what the voice if you will is telling me to share with anyone that wants to hear it!
"Death is a word, non descriptive of the truth, there is no true death when it comes to YOU, or anyone's soul!"
That is all!
There is more tho, however this is for Rob, the message following:
I LOVE YOU PUMPKIN?
Don't worry, I'm alright and waiting for you, you'll be fine, thank you!
That is all!
Blessings Upon You All ❤

So to that end, Robert: my heart goes out to you; I wish you and all those who knew and loved her to allow grief to do its work, and that each of you comes out the other side of this transition better for it.

author: @HeadakusMaximus
text:
I lost my life companion, of a mere ten years, five months ago. I am just now returning to a functional state. So I can't imagine how much worse your pain is. 

Please remember that we also need you. Your face, voice, and knowledge gives us hope, happiness, and inspires our curiosity. Patti is outside of the timestream now. Someday you will join her. Take a bit of time to grieve and heal, but don't let too much time pass before you lift your head again and march forward. You have people to lead.

author: @trplankowner71
text:
My fullest condolences Rob. There are no words that can be said to relieve the pain. However, I will say this, the one thing that people can do for someone that has passed is what you've done here; remember the good things about that person, recall and recount the things that prove that their time here made a difference and meant something to others.
I'm praying for Patti and you; Rob and I will be here if and when you choose to make more content. Should you choose not to, you can rest assured that your body of work is more than enough accomplishment for any one man. 
Rest in peace Patti.

author: @michaellinahan7740
text: Robert please accept my condolences for your loss and make sure that you and the family have someone to talk to and work through the grieving process; I have been there too many times in the last 5 years having lost 5 immediate family members to pancreatic cancer and my eldest brother having just been given a stage IV diagnosis of the same thing; a foul disease that is not supposed to have familial links. One of my biggest regrets is that I was not clever enough to contribute to the research in the medical field nor earned enough as a teacher of maths, physics and control technology for 45 years to donate the vast funding required to research a cure for cancer. I can only hope that I have inspired a student to take up the mantle that I, sadly, could not.

author: @shawncalderon4950
text:
I am so very sorry for your loss. There aren't words for the heartfelt compassion I feel for you. When my adult daughter was murdered two years ago, I read every grief book I could get my hands on. Very few of the books were helpful to me. Here are two books that helped me the most. 1. Grieving with Hope by Samuel Hodges and Kathy Leonard.   2. Traveling through Grief by Susan J. Zonnebelt-Smeenge and Dr. Robert C. De Vries.
Find a GriefShare group near you. This will surround you with experienced people who will comfort and love on you.
You are now in the new normal.
All firsts will hurt. First drive to fill the car with petrol. The first visit to the store. The first visit to the movie theater. The first Halloween or Christmas or other holidays. 
You will endure more pain than you could imagine.
The agony will last longer than you can comprehend.
The days will be long, the years short.
The pain does go away.
The pain will change.
You will be happy again.
There is a purpose in your suffering, and you aren't alone.

I will add you to my prayer list.
Much tenderness, Professor Murray.

replies.author: @TomPutty
replies.text:
I remember this place of mourning all too well, kind sir. These condolences exist because I can commiserate with the loss of a beloved bride. Yes, the first 2 years are tough, and yes I remember her fondly and in vivid detail now 25 years later, and while my heart still feels that sense of loss, as if part of me has been missing all this time, there was still joy to be found over these years enjoying the smiles of other family members, and in simply tinkering. 

God is good, dear sir, and a true anchor in life's ugliest storms.

author: @roningelert
text:
“Do not stand at my grave and weep;
I am not there. I do not sleep.
I am a thousand winds that blow.
I am the diamond glints on snow.
I am the sunlight on ripened grain.
I am the gentle autumn rain.
When you awaken in the morning's hush
I am the swift uplifting rush
Of quiet birds in circled flight.
I am the soft stars that shine at night.
Do not stand at my grave and cry;
I am not there.
I did not die.”

replies.author: @Brown_Potato
replies.text:
Robert (if it is okay to use your first name) you give me inspiration for ideas and dreams that I have long lost since entering college, graduation, and taking on my first job. Thank you for everything that you have done and for what is to come in the future. I do hope you find a pathway to peace after this, and I hope the best for everyone around you, especially yourself.

I am a young physicist and it is impossible to state how much creative juices you get flowing in my head, never have I had this much respect and admiration for someone who presents interesting topics and tinkering. You keep it straight to the point, present interesting stories, and have an infectious joy for tinkering. 

Please take care
